title_en: 5. Patience at times of fitnah when the Muslims are fighting Muslims. Patience in
title_id: 5. Kesabaran di saat fitnah ketika kaum Muslim saling berperang. Kesabaran dalam
content_en:
abstaining from fighting at such a time, when Muslims are killing Muslims, is mubâh (permissible), indeed it is mustahabb (liked and preferred). When the Prophet (SAAS) was asked about this, he said, "Be like the better of the two sons of Ādam." In other, similar reports he said, "Be like the slave of Allâh who was killed, and not like the one who has killed," and "let him (the killer) carry his own wrong action and your wrong action." In another report, he said, "If the sword is too bright, put your hand on your face." Allâh has told us the story of the better of the two sons of Ādam, and how he surrendered himself and did not fight back, and how Allâh commended him for that. This

is different to the case when Muslims are fighting kâfirûn: in that situation the Muslim has to defend himself, because the meaning of Jihad is to defend himself and Islâm. Disliked (makrûh) patience
content_id:
menahan diri dari berperang pada saat seperti itu, ketika kaum Muslim membunuh kaum Muslim, adalah mubah (diperbolehkan), bahkan itu adalah mustahab (disukai dan lebih diutamakan). Ketika Nabi ﷺ ditanya tentang hal ini, beliau bersabda, "Jadilah seperti yang lebih baik dari dua putra Ādam." Dalam riwayat lain yang serupa beliau bersabda, "Jadilah seperti hamba Allah yang terbunuh, bukan seperti yang membunuh," dan "biarkan dia (pembunuh) menanggung kesalahannya sendiri dan kesalahanmu." Dalam riwayat lain, beliau bersabda, "Jika pedang terlalu terang, letakkan tanganmu di wajahmu." Allah telah menceritakan kepada kita kisah yang lebih baik dari dua putra Ādam, dan bagaimana ia menyerahkan dirinya dan tidak melawan, dan bagaimana Allah memujinya atas hal itu. Ini

berbeda dengan kasus ketika kaum Muslim berperang melawan orang-orang kafir: dalam situasi itu seorang Muslim harus membela dirinya, karena makna Jihad adalah membela dirinya dan Islam. Kesabaran yang dibenci (makruh)
content_ar: abstaining from fighting at such a time, عندما المسلمون are killing المسلمون, is mubâh (permissible), indeed it is المستحب (liked and preferred). عندما النبي ﷺ (SAAS) was asked about this, قال, "Be like the better of the two sons of Ādam." In other, similar reports قال, "Be like the slave of Allâh who was killed, and not like the one who has killed," and "let him (the killer) carry his own الخطأ action and your الخطأ action." In another report, قال, "إذا the sword is أيضاً bright, put your hand on your face." Allâh has told us the story of the better of the two sons of Ādam, and how he surrendered himself and did not fight back, and how Allâh commended him for that. This is different to the case عندما المسلمون are fighting kâfirûn: in that situation the مسلم has to defend himself, لأن the meaning of Jihad is to defend himself and Islâm. Disliked (makrûh) الصبر
